content: Just as the medial prefrontal cortex and hippocampus interact to retrieve and stabilize context-rich associative memories of past exploration, a 2025-configured ICM curiosity module for autonomous agents can be redesigned to incorporate a prefrontal-hippocampal interaction dynamic: the ICM’s intrinsic reward signal acts like the mPFC to prioritize retrieval of novel associative memory traces stored by a hippocampus-like rapid context-binding module, then gradually stabilizes high-value exploratory memory traces to guide future curious behavior. This hierarchical interactive memory architecture solves a longstanding ICM limitation of wasted exploration on fully unlearnable or randomly noisy novel environments.
